member.name: Jamie Stone
member.role: MP for Caithness, Sutherland and Easter Ross

addresses: Hillview, Market Street, Tain, Ross Shire, IV19 1AR
Office 2, Norcad Design, George Street, Wick, Caithness, KW1 4DG

member.synopsis: Jamie Stone is the Liberal Democrat MP for Caithness, Sutherland and Easter Ross , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Peatlands: Fire Prevention
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the scientific evidence on rewetting peatland and other habitats and resilience to wildfire; and what her Department's strategy is for reduci
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of its delivery of the Third National Adaptation Programme, Defra has commissioned a wildfire and peatland study, which includes looking at the impact of peat restoration on building wildfire resilience into the landscape. The Government recognise...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether his Department plans to support further assessment of Dounreay as a site for future nuclear deployment following the publication of Great British Energy – Nuclear's Technical Advice o
writtenQuestions.answerText: Great British Energy – Nuclear's assessment concluded that parts of Scotland, including areas around Dounreay, have technical potential for future new nuclear development. However, planning policy is devolved to the Scottish Government. Their current poli...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Dounreay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, with reference to Great British Energy – Nuclear's Technical Advice on Potential Future Nuclear Power Plant Siting in Scotland report, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of n
writtenQuestions.answerText: GBE N's study of Scotland's potential for new nuclear indicates that it has land areas with high potential for future development. New nuclear projects can deliver billions of pounds of investment and thousands of high-quality jobs to a region. While Scot...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Dounreay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, with reference to Great British Energy – Nuclear's Technical Advice on Potential Future Nuclear Power Plant Siting in Scotland report, what discussions he has had with the Scottish Government
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers commissioned GBE N's study of Scotland's potential for new nuclear last autumn. The study indicates that Scotland has land areas with high potential for future development. We remain open to discussions with the Scottish Government regarding opp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Service Broadcasting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, if she will review the public service broadcasting framework to increase protections for local and regional news provision.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While the Government recognises STV’s operational independence, it is important that they continue to provide for all audiences in Northern and Central Scotland. I have recently engaged with STV senior leadership to communicate my disappointment with this...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Broadcasting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what discussions she has had with Ofcom on ensuring that changes to regional broadcasting licences do not lead to a disproportionate concentration of news production in central urban areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While the Government recognises STV’s operational independence, it is important that they continue to provide for all audiences in Northern and Central Scotland. I have recently engaged with STV senior leadership to communicate my disappointment with this...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Service Broadcasting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what plans her Department has to ensure that distinct local voices continue to be represented in public service broadcasting.

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether she has made an assessment of the potential impact on local democratic engagement and accountability of reductions in dedicated regional news services in the north of Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While the Government recognises STV’s operational independence, it is important that they continue to provide for all audiences in Northern and Central Scotland. I have recently engaged with STV senior leadership to communicate my disappointment with this...

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Broadcasting: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to ensure that viewers in areas such as the Highlands and Islands are not impacted by the centralisation of broadcast news production.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While the Government recognises STV’s operational independence, it is important that they continue to provide for all audiences in Northern and Central Scotland. I have recently engaged with STV senior leadership to communicate my disappointment with this...

writtenQuestions.answerText: While the Government recognises STV’s operational independence, it is important that they continue to provide for all audiences in Northern and Central Scotland. I have recently engaged with STV senior leadership to communicate my disappointment with this...

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Dounreay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, in reference to Great British Energy – Nuclear’s large-scale nuclear siting study which includes Scottish sites, what nuclear technologies have been identified as suitable for deployment at D
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government has tasked Great British Energy – Nuclear (GBE-N) with identifying suitable sites, including those in Scotland, that could host a potential large-scale new nuclear project. GBE-N will report to Ministers by Autumn 2026 on potential sites to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to help tackle strategic issues affecting domestic air travel, including (a) airport capacity and regional connectivity, (b) the resilience and availability of aircraft, (c) the regulat
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the vital role of domestic aviation in supporting economic growth and regional connectivity. The sector operates largely on a commercial basis, with airports and airlines best placed to determine capacity and routes. It is also t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Councils: Insurance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether their Department intends to explore mechanisms, such as pooled schemes, indemnity funds, or shared-risk arrangements, to ensure that Community Councils can access protect
writtenQuestions.answerText: Community Councils in Scotland are statutory bodies established under devolved Scottish legislation, and responsibility for their governance, funding arrangements and related guidance rests with the Scottish Government and Scottish local authorities. As t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammonia: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether his Department intends to develop a standard for low carbon ammonia.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ammonia can serve as an essential feedstock across a wide range of industrial applications and could support decarbonisation in hard-to-electrify transport sectors, such as maritime. Ammonia can also be used as a ‘hydrogen carrier’ to store, transport an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammonia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what estimate he has made of the near-term and long-term i) demand for and ii) supply of ammonia in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ammonia can serve as an essential feedstock across a wide range of industrial applications and could support decarbonisation in hard-to-electrify transport sectors, such as maritime. Ammonia can also be used as a ‘hydrogen carrier’ to store, transport and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammonia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether he has made an assessment of the potential merits of providing revenue support for ammonia-to-power projects (a) under the Hydrogen to Production Business Model and (b) through a besp
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Hydrogen Production Business Model provides revenue support to selected low carbon hydrogen producers who may sell that hydrogen for a variety of applications including as a feedstock for the production of ammonia. The Government’s Hydrogen to Power b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammonia: Storage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of low-carbon ammonia as a flexible energy store, including to provide seasonal energy storage and dispatchable power.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government recognises that ammonia could play an important role for the transport, storage andtrade of hydrogen, especially for hydrogen users with limited access to networks. Ammonia based chemical storage for cracking hydrogen was assessed to have a low...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammonia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential implications of low-carbon ammonia for UK energy security, in the context of rising natural gas prices linked to the US-Israel conflict with Iran.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The biggest threat to energy security for families and businesses in the UK is continued reliance on unstable fossil fuel markets. That is why we’re taking back control with clean, homegrown power to ensure our energy security, protect the British people ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
